summary: High‑energy desert set with stellar Here Comes Sunshine and jam‑heavy second half
blurb: The show opens with a soaring Here Comes Sunshine and weaves through classics like Althea, Greatest Story Ever Told and Deal. The second set builds into powerful jams on Uncle John's Band, The Last Time, China Doll and Throwing Stones before a heartfelt Brokedown Palace encore. Jerry’s playing is bright and adventurous while Vince’s keyboards add texture throughout.
review:
December 6, 1992 at Compton Terrace Amphitheatre delivered one of the most vibrant desert shows of the early ’90s. From the first note of "Here Comes Sunshine" the band was on a roll – Jerry’s tone was crisp and his vocals surprisingly youthful, Bob’s guitar cut through with gritty intensity on tracks like Althea and Throwing Stones, and Vince Welnick painted the soundscape with organic piano/organ colors that never overwhelmed. The second set was a highlight reel of jam‑centric moments: a soaring "Uncle John's Band" segued into an expansive "Playing In The Band", then into the rhythmic storm of Drums → Space before landing on the emotional climax of "The Last Time" and the haunting beauty of "China Doll". The night closed with a warm, sing‑along "Brokedown Palace" that captured the desert’s twilight vibe.

Across the six available recordings the consensus is clear – this performance shines despite a few vocal rough spots and occasional mic hiccups. The Wiley soundboard (gd92-12-06.sbd.wiley.8706.sbeok.shnf) offers the cleanest mix, capturing Bobby’s front‑center guitar and Vince’s textures with clarity while preserving the live energy. Whether you listen to the pristine SBD or the excellent audience tape that lets you feel the crowd’s roar, this show stands out as a must‑hear snapshot of the Dead’s late‑period brilliance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six recordings are described with positive sentiment, repeatedly praising the high‑energy performance, standout songs (Here Comes Sunshine, Uncle John's Band, The Last Time, etc.), and strong playing from Jerry, Bob, Vince and the rhythm section. No consistent negative criticism of the musical execution appears; only minor vocal or transition hiccups are noted. This uniform enthusiasm warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Clean soundboard mix that captures the full band balance, crisp guitar and keyboards, and overall high‑energy desert vibe with only minor technical glitches.

key_highlights: Opening "Here Comes Sunshine" sets an uplifting tone for the night.
"Uncle John's Band" → "Playing In The Band" → Drums/Space → "The Last Time" sequence delivers a peak second‑set jam.
Vince’s tasteful keyboard textures add depth without overpowering the mix.
Bobby’s gritty guitar shines on Althea and Throwing Stones, while Jerry plays bright and adventurous.

band_performance.Jerry: Plays with a happy, healthy tone and adventurous phrasing; vocals stay soulful despite occasional roughness later in the set.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid bass foundation throughout, highlighted on his verse in "Maggie's Farm" and maintaining steady groove.
band_performance.Bob: Guitar is prominent and gritty, delivering strong leads on Althea and Throwing Stones; occasional lyrical slip‑ups but overall powerful tone.
band_performance.Brent: Vince's keyboards are organic and tasteful, adding texture to jams and the "Greatest Story" segment without overwhelming the mix.
band_performance.Mickey: Adds tribal conga flavor and solid rhythmic support, especially during the Space/Last Time passages.
band_performance.Billy: Drums drive the set with intensity and fun, delivering strong backbeat on the second‑set jams.
